t: Building a Full Screen Top Shelf Extension
a: Highlight content from your Apple TV application by building a full screen Top Shelf extension.
Help users discover your app by providing top shelf content and a description of your tvOS app.

t: Mapping Apple TV users to app profiles
a: Adapt the content of your app for the current viewer by using an entitlement and simplifying sign-in flows.

t: Personalizing Your App for Each User on Apple TV
a: Use account-specific storage to segregate data on a multiuser system.
Support browsing an electronic program guide (EPG) and changing channels with specialized remote buttons.

t: Supporting Multiple Users in Your tvOS App
a: Store separate data for each user with the new Runs as Current User capability.
Display content and descriptions, provide channel guides, and support multiple users on Apple TV.
A model object that you use to represent an app-specific profile.
An object that uniquely identifies media content in either a single piece or a collection.
An object that describes either a piece of content or a container for other content items.
An enumerated type that identifies the shape in which the content item should be displayed.
The content is high-definition television content with a width:height ratio of `16:9`.
The content is standard-definition television content with a width:height ratio of `4:3`.
The content has a width:height ratio of `80:27`.
The content has no particular shape.
The content has a width:height ratio of `2:3`.
The content has a width:height ratio of `1:1`.
The content has a width:height ratio of `8:3`.
Traits describing the type of image you want.
An action to perform in response to user interactions with an item in the top shelf.
A set of items you present using a carousel-style interface in the top shelf.
Constants indicating how you want items to appear in the top shelf interface.
An interface that focuses on the actions you want the user to perform.
An interface that displays detailed information about the item.
An item containing images, video, and other information that you want to display using a carousel-based interface.
Constants indicating the item’s audio and video capabilities.
The protocol that objects adopt to provide content for the top shelf.
The main interface for your Top Shelf app extension, which you use to provide content for the top shelf area of the tvOS Home Screen.
An enumerated type used to specify the style in which you want your content to be displayed.

When the using the inset style, your extension should return a flat array of TV content items. The images of the content items will take up most of the area of the Top Shelf, which will slowly rotate through the items.

When using the sectioned style, your extension should return an array that contains content items that represent sections. Each section object should have an array of content items that represent the available media in that section.
Returns the ideal size for an image, according to its particular shape and style.
A set of items to present using an inset-style interface in the top shelf.
An item that uses an image to represent a movie, show, or other content in the top shelf.
Constants describing the image format.
A group of items that you display together in a sectioned interface in the top shelf.
A notification to post when your app’s Top Shelf content has changed.
An object you use to display additional information.
An abstract base class for describing top shelf items and item collections.
The interface for providing items to display in the main menu’s Top Shelf user interface on an Apple TV.
The set of items you want to present using a section-based interface in the top shelf.
